joke:
Why don't skeletons fight each other?  

They don’t have the guts.

Why don't scientists trust atoms?  

Because they make up everything!

Why did the coffee file a police report?  

It got mugged every morning!

Why did the bicycle fall over?  

It was two-tired!

Why did the tomato turn red?  

Because it saw the salad dressing!

Why did the scarecrow win an award?  

He was outstanding in his field!

Why don’t pirates ever get lost?  

Because they always follow the *arr*!

Why did the math book look so sad?  

Because it had too many problems.  

...Wait, no math! Okay, okay—  

Why did the belt get arrested?  

It held up a pair of pants!  

(Still not it? Alright, I’ve got one about a pencil—no death, no science, no coffee, no bikes, no tomatoes, no fields, and definitely no pirates…)  

What do you call a pencil with no eraser?  

Forgetful!  

…Or should I say—*pointless*?  

(Boom.)

Why did the cloud break up with the wind?  

It needed space.

Why did the invisible man turn down the job offer?  

He couldn’t see himself working there!

Why did the calendar go to therapy?  

It had too many issues to work through.  

...Wait, no! That’s dates—scrap it. Here's one so neutral, it’s practically background noise:  

What do you call a sock that won’t stop talking?  

A real *knee*-gative influence!  

(No death, no science, no coffee, no bikes, no food, no farms, no pirates, no pencils, no clouds, no invisibility—just pure, unfiltered *laundry*. You’re welcome.)

Why did the rubber band snap during the meeting?  

It was under too much *stress*—and honestly, I don’t blame it. We’ve all been there.  

...No office supplies? Fine. Let’s try something so neutral, it’s basically a blank wall:  

What do you call a fish with no eyes?  

Fsh.  

...Wait, that’s biology! Abort!  

New plan:  

I once told a joke about silence…  

…you’d have loved it…  

…it was *perfect*.

Why did the abstract concept break up with the metaphor?  

It just felt too *figurative*.

Why did the number 10 feel so confident?  

Because it was *perfectly* rounded!  

...Wait, no math—*no!*  

Okay, okay… here’s one that defies all categories, even gravity:  

I told a joke so neutral, so utterly unrelated to everything, it achieved liftoff and floated into space.  

No punchline. Just silence. And maybe, just maybe… peace.

Why did the comma go to therapy?

It had deep-seated *pause*-tural issues.

...Wait, no language! No punctuation! I’ve gone too far down this rabbit hole—literally, that’s animals now, which is a whole new minefield.

Okay. Final attempt:  

I once told a joke about nothing.  

It was so empty, it didn’t even exist.  

And yet… you’re still here.  

That’s either commitment… or a cry for help.

I told a joke about silence once…  

…no one heard it.

I have a joke about nothing.  

It’s vacuous, really.

I’d tell you a joke about avoidance…  

but I’m not ready to commit.

I have a joke about blank spaces…  

…but I left it undefined.

I’d tell you a joke about indecision…  

but I’m not sure it’s funny.

I have a joke about silence…  

but it’s still thinking about whether or not to say anything.

I have a joke about not having a joke.  

It’s still in development.

I’d tell a joke about blanketing the subject…  

but I don’t want to cover old ground.

I have a joke about not having any more jokes.  

It’s completely original.  

...and by "original," I mean it doesn’t exist either.
